item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hi, I’m Haeji Ahn!

I am a graduate student in the MS-CAPP program at the University of Chicago with a Master’s degree in Criminology. I focus on technical data solutions and analytical modeling to address complex social and urban challenges. My background in Criminology provides me with a specialized perspective on social data, allowing for deeper contextual analysis of the models and systems I build.

I am passionate about managing the entire data lifecycle and am pursuing career opportunities: DS or DE.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
I am always eager to collaborate on impactful data projects or discuss new opportunities in social policy and technology. Whether you have a question or just want to say hi, my inbox is always open.

  • Email: ahaeji925@uchicago.edu
  • LinkedIn: LinkedIn
  • Resume: View resume here

item.title: Publications
item.description:
I have authored three peer-reviewed publications and have been invited as a featured speaker at several prestigious academic conferences, bridging the gap between criminological theory and data-driven insights.

📚 Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles

  • Factors Influencing Smart Phone and Game Addiction among School-Dropout Adolescents: Focusing on Gender Differences

    • Ahn, H., & Lee, C. | Korean Association of Addiction Crime Review, 13(4)
    • [ Link ] [ Scholar ]

  • The Effect of Violence Allowance on Dating Violation Perpetration: The Moderating Effect of Self-Esteem

item.title: VR Simulation Workflow Design
item.description:
Design of VR Simulations for Law Enforcement & Public Safety

This project focuses on creating immersive virtual environments to train law enforcement officers and public safety personnel in high-stress decision-making.

🔍 Project Overview

By integrating Criminology and Crime Science theories, I designed high-fidelity VR scenarios that replicate real-world legal and tactical challenges. I translated complex legal protocols into intuitive virtual workflows for effective situational training.

  • Scenario Design: Developed the “Initial Investigation & Convenience Store Disturbance” VR scenario, focusing on legal compliance and officer safety.
  • Behavioral Modeling: Incorporated criminal behavior patterns and victimology into the simulation to enhance environmental realism.
  • Technical Specification: Authored technical requirements for VR developers to ensure the simulation maintains high procedural fidelity.

🛠 Key Contributions

  • Academic Research: Leveraged findings from the Journal of SMART Policing to build data-backed virtual training modules.
  • Subject Matter Expertise: Bridged the communication gap between law enforcement experts and VR engineers to ensure accurate protocol implementation.
  • UX/UI for Training: Designed user interfaces within the VR space that allow for the collection of quantitative trainee response data.

item.title: XR: Smart Policing Simulation
item.description:
Development of XR Training Scenarios for Law Enforcement

This project focuses on bridging the gap between criminological theory and immersive technology by designing high-fidelity training simulations for public safety.

🔍 Project Overview

Based on my research published in the Journal of SMART Policing, I developed system workflows and evaluation tools for extended reality (XR) training. I translated complex legal protocols and criminal behavior patterns into technical specifications to create realistic response scenarios.
